0.response: {"Q0":"The strategy I adopted was sticking to the rules and feeling contempt with trying to collect as many gems possible, even if that meant risking uncovering rocks."}

7.response: {"Q0":"Overall, the chance to best maximize potential gold and money possible."}

9.response: {"Q0":"Overall the instructions were clear, there was a lot to process and I had to do the comprehension questions more than once, but they were easy enough to understand how the game worked. "}

1.response: {"Q0":"My overall strategy stayed the same throughout the game, I was very consistent in that regard. "}

5.response: {"Q0":"No, it was made very clear the blue planet no longer had an astronaut and any gems recovered would not be able to be sold. "}

2.response: {"Q0":"I would say at least something around the 70% mark was successful "}

6.response: {"Q0":"When it was clear the astronaut on the blue planet had left, any meteor shower that came after the fact I exclusively focused on selecting red gems only. "}

8.response: {"Q0":"I would say it was an almost equal focus, paying more attention to not dig on wrong planets and getting the order right was more of a primary focus, the galaxies went by so fast I found they were more split reaction choices. "}

3.response: {"Q0":"Yes, I knew right away to then avoid attempting to collect any gem after this occurred. "}

4.response: {"Q0":"Yes, reading the announcement from the astronaut was extremely clear, afterwards I knew to not dig on the blue planet for the remainder of the game. "}

11.response: {"Q0":"It was a challenging but very enjoyable game. I loved taking part  :)"}
